_comment: This is an INTENTIONALLY EMPTY, honest ground-truth template. It contains
NO vulnerability entries. Real file:line locations must be populated by a
human who has actually cloned and inspected the real OWASP NodeGoat repo.
Fabricating plausible-sounding NodeGoat vulnerability locations from
training-data memory (without an explicit verification step against the
real repository) would violate this codebase's 'never fabricate confidence'
discipline -- the same rule the runtime report generator follows. An empty
template honestly scores every discovered finding as a false positive,
which is the correct outcome of 'we have not verified any ground truth
yet', rather than an invented set of locations. See README.md in this
directory for how to populate it and how each entry must be verified.

_entry_schema_example.app_path: <repo-relative path to the vulnerable app root, e.g. 'app'>
_entry_schema_example.line: <1-based line number of the vulnerable sink, e.g. 32>
_entry_schema_example.cvss_4_0_vector: <CVSS 4.0 vector string, or null when not assigned>
_entry_schema_example._verified_by: <how this entry was verified: who inspected which commit, e.g. 'inspected NodeGoat @ <sha> on 2026-07-02'>
